sections.title: PRODUCT DEFINITION
sections.text: Dried shark fins are the dorsal and pectoral fins cut in the form of an arc and the lower lobe of the caudal fin cut straight, from which all flesh has been removed, and are cut from species of sharks which are safe for human consumption.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=2.1
level=2

sections.title: PROCESS DEFINITION
sections.text: The fins shall be subjected to a drying process so as to meet the requirements of Section 3.2.4 and shall comply with the conditions laid down hereafter.

sections.title: SHARK
sections.text: Dried shark fins shall be prepared from sound sharks which are of a quality fit to be sold fresh for human consumption.

sections.title: Other Forms of Presentation
sections.text: Any other presentation shall be permitted provided that it: (i) meets all other requirements of this standard; and (ii) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination prescribed by the Codex Alimentarius Commission, the product:
path_titles: When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination prescribed by the Codex Alimentarius Commission, the product:
sections.text: (i) shall be free from microorganisms or substances originating from microorganisms in amounts which may present a hazard to health in accordance with standards established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission ; (ii) shall not contain any other substance in amounts which  may present a hazard to health in accordance with standards established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: NAME OF THE FOOD
sections.text: The name of the product shall be "dried shark fins" or any other appropriate name in accordance with the law and custom of the country in which the product is to be distributed.

sections.title: LABELLING OF NON-RETAIL CONTAINERS
sections.text: Information on the above provisions shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer, shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sections.title: SAMPLING
sections.text: (i) Sampling of lots for examination of the product shall be in accordance with an appropiate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5; (ii) The sampling of lots for examination of net weight shall be carried out according to the Codex Sampling Plans for the Determination of Net Weight (under elaboration).

sections.title: SENSORY AND PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
sections.text: Samples taken for sensory and physical examination shall be assessed by persons trained in such examination and in accordance with the procedures set out in Section 7.3, Annex B "Sensoric and Physical Examination"1 and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CAC/GL 31 - 1999).
sections.retrieval_text:
SENSORY AND PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
number=7.2
level=1
Samples taken for sensory and physical examination shall be assessed by persons trained in such examination and in accordance with the procedures set out in Section 7.3, Annex B "Sensoric and Physical Examination"1 and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CAC/GL 31 - 1999).

page_spans.text: Samples taken for sensory and physical examination shall be assessed by persons trained in such examination and in accordance with the procedures set out in Section 7.3, Annex B "Sensoric and Physical Examination"1 and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CAC/GL 31 - 1999).

sections.title: LOT ACCEPTANCE
sections.text: A lot shall be considered as meeting the requirements of this Standard when: (i) the total number of defectiv es as classified according to Section 8 does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5; (ii) the average net weight of all sample units is not less than the declared weight, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in any container; and (iii) the total number of sample units not meeting the form of presentation as defined in Section 2.3 does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5; (iv) the Food Additive, Hygiene and Handling and Labelling requirements of Sections 4, 5.1, 5.2 and 6 are met.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=9
level=1
A lot shall be considered as meeting the requirements of this Standard when: (i) the total number of defectiv es as classified according to Section 8 does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5; (ii) the average net weight of all sample units is not less than the declared weight, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in any container; and (iii) the total number of sample units not meeting the form of presentation as defined in Section 2.3 does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5; (iv) the Food Additive, Hygiene and Handling and Labelling requirements of Sections 4, 5.1, 5.2 and 6 are met.

sections.title: FOREIGN MATTER
sections.text: The presence in the sample unit of any matter which has not been derived from fish, does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determine d by any method including magnification that indicates non -compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.

sentences.original: Dried shark fins are the dorsal and pectoral fins cut in the form of an arc and the lower lobe of the caudal fin cut straight, from which all flesh has been removed, and are cut from species of sharks which are safe for human consumption.

prefilter.reason: Describes specific product and safety pertinent to regulation.

classification.sentence: Dried shark fins are the dorsal and pectoral fins cut in the form of an arc and the lower lobe of the caudal fin cut straight, from which all flesh has been removed, and are cut from species of sharks which are safe for human consumption.

classification.justification: The sentence defines the term and includes detailed compositional and source information; a definitional pattern without normative modality.
notes: Extended detailed description makes it unique.

sentences.original: A lot shall be considered as meeting the requirements of this Standard when: (i) the total number of defectiv es as classified according to Section 8 does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5;

classification.sentence: (ii) the average net weight of all sample units is not less than the declared weight, provided there is no unreasonable shortage in any container; and (iii) the total number of sample units not meeting the form of presentation as defined in Section 2.3 does not exceed the acceptance number (c) of an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5; (iv) the Food Additive, Hygiene and Handling and Labelling requirements of Sections 4, 5.1, 5.2 and 6 are met.
classification.pattern: Enumerated conditions linked by conjunctions

components.condition_4: Food Additive, Hygiene and Handling and Labelling requirements met

classification.justification: Multiple enumerated conditions defining conformity; complex structure and multiple conditions prompt adaptation classification.
notes: Continued conditions referring to lot conformity.

sentences.original: The presence in the sample unit of any matter which has not been derived from fish, does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determine d by any method including magnification that indicates non -compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.

prefilter.reason: Contains regulation-related language about sample analysis.

classification.sentence: The presence in the sample unit of any matter which has not been derived from fish, does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determine d by any method including magnification that indicates non -compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.
classification.pattern: Subject + presence + qualifiers + negative health risk + recognizability + compliance indication

components.subject: presence in sample unit of any matter not derived from fish
components.health_risk: does not pose a threat to human health
components.recognizability: readily recognized without magnification or present at a level determined by any method including magnification
components.non_compliance: indicates non-compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices

classification.justification: Complex conditional descriptive sentence reflecting a compound condition involving presence, risk, recognizability and GMP compliance; structure and modality do not conform clearly to known templates.
notes: Complex multi-part conditional sentence with mixed descriptive and normative elements.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR DRIED SHARK FINS
pages.summary: Página com hierarquia clara: documento sobre padrão para nadadeiras secas de tubarão. Encontrados títulos de nível 1, 2 e 3 com numeração. Texto associado está limpo e preserva a estrutura original. Cabeçalho identificado. Sem continuidade entre páginas.

Página apresenta cabeçalho identificado e hierarquia clara com níveis 1 a 3. Seções 4 a 7 estruturadas com seus respectivos subtítulos numerados. Texto associado está limpo e preserva a estrutura original. A seção 6 continua na próxima página, marcada com continues_on_next_page=true.

pages.title: CODEX STAN 189-1993
pages.summary: Página 3 do documento CODEX STAN 189-1993, apresenta hierarquia principal de seções numeradas de 7.2 a 9 com níveis 1 e 2. A seção 7.2 continua da página anterior, marcada como is_continuation_from_previous_page true. Texto está bem associado aos seus títulos, com subtítulos claros na seção 8. A página contém cabeçalho repetitivo removido do texto interno. Continuidade entre páginas foi considerada para a seção 7.2.

A página 4 do documento apresenta um título em caixa alta sem numeração indicando 'ANNEX A' como nível 1. Não há texto associado ou subtítulos na página. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e removido do texto interno. Não há indicações de continuidade a partir ou para outras páginas nesta seção.
